title: AcyclicPreservedUnderDo
lean_code_with_comments:
theorem acyclic_preserved_under_do (G : CDMG Node) (W : Finset Node)
    (hW : W ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V) :
    G.IsAcyclic →
      (G.hardInterventionOn W hW).IsAcyclic ∧
        (∀ lt : Node → Node → Prop,
          G.IsTopologicalOrder lt →
            (G.hardInterventionOn W hW).IsTopologicalOrder lt)
-- claim_3_3 -- end statement
:= by
  intro hAcyclic
  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
  -- (a) Acyclicity preservation: if a non-trivial directed walk
  --     `v ⟶⋯⟶ v` existed in `G.hardInterventionOn W hW`, the
  --     walk-lift transfers it -- as the same tuple, by step (0) of
  --     the TeX proof -- to a non-trivial directed walk in `G` at
  --     the same `v`, contradicting `G.IsAcyclic`.
  · intro v hv
    rintro ⟨p, hp_dir, hp_len⟩
    have hv_G : v ∈ G := mem_of_mem_hardInterventionOn hv
    apply hAcyclic v hv_G
    refine ⟨Walk.liftFromHardIntervention p,
            Walk.isDirectedWalk_liftFromHardIntervention p hp_dir, ?_⟩
    rw [Walk.length_liftFromHardIntervention p]
    exact hp_len
  -- (b) Topological-order preservation: each conjunct of
  --     `(G.hardInterventionOn W hW).IsTopologicalOrder lt` transfers
  --     from the corresponding conjunct of `G.IsTopologicalOrder lt`
  --     via the carrier-matching equality (for the strict-total-order
  --     conjuncts, which quantify over `v ∈ G`) and the edge-inclusion
  --     `(G.hardInterventionOn W hW).E ⊆ G.E` (for the
  --     parent-precedence conjunct, which gives
  --     `Pa^{G_{do(W)}}(w) ⊆ Pa^G(w)` pointwise).  The acyclicity
  --     hypothesis `hAcyclic` is NOT used in this branch -- sub-claim
  --     (b) holds without it (the proof in the TeX's closing remark);
  --     we keep `hAcyclic` bound at the theorem head purely for
  --     LN-faithfulness of the packaging.
  · intro lt hTop
    obtain ⟨⟨h_irrefl, h_trans, h_tricho⟩, h_parent⟩ := hTop
    refine ⟨⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩, ?_⟩
    · -- Irreflexivity on the intervened carrier
      intro v hv
      exact h_irrefl v (mem_of_mem_hardInterventionOn hv)
    · -- Transitivity on the intervened carrier
      intro u hu v hv w hw hluv hlvw
      exact h_trans u (mem_of_mem_hardInterventionOn hu)
                    v (mem_of_mem_hardInterventionOn hv)
                    w (mem_of_mem_hardInterventionOn hw)
                    hluv hlvw
    · -- Trichotomy on the intervened carrier
      intro v hv w hw
      exact h_tricho v (mem_of_mem_hardInterventionOn hv)
                     w (mem_of_mem_hardInterventionOn hw)
    · -- Parent-precedence on `G.hardInterventionOn W hW`: from
      --   `v ∈ (G.hardInterventionOn W hW).Pa w` we extract
      --   `v ∈ G.hardInterventionOn W hW` and
      --   `(v, w) ∈ (G.hardInterventionOn W hW).E`; the former lifts
      --   to `v ∈ G` via `mem_of_mem_hardInterventionOn` and the
      --   latter lifts to `(v, w) ∈ G.E` via `Finset.mem_filter`.
      --   Together they give `v ∈ G.Pa w`, so `h_parent` yields
      --   `lt v w`.
      intro v w hvPa
      obtain ⟨hv_G', hvw_E'⟩ := hvPa
      have hvw_E_G : (v, w) ∈ G.E := (Finset.mem_filter.mp hvw_E').1
      have hv_G : v ∈ G := mem_of_mem_hardInterventionOn hv_G'
      exact h_parent v w ⟨hv_G, hvw_E_G⟩
lean_code_without_comments:
theorem acyclic_preserved_under_do (G : CDMG Node) (W : Finset Node)
    (hW : W ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V) :
    G.IsAcyclic →
      (G.hardInterventionOn W hW).IsAcyclic ∧
        (∀ lt : Node → Node → Prop,
          G.IsTopologicalOrder lt →
            (G.hardInterventionOn W hW).IsTopologicalOrder lt)

:= by
  intro hAcyclic
  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩

  · intro v hv
    rintro ⟨p, hp_dir, hp_len⟩
    have hv_G : v ∈ G := mem_of_mem_hardInterventionOn hv
    apply hAcyclic v hv_G
    refine ⟨Walk.liftFromHardIntervention p,
            Walk.isDirectedWalk_liftFromHardIntervention p hp_dir, ?_⟩
    rw [Walk.length_liftFromHardIntervention p]
    exact hp_len

  · intro lt hTop
    obtain ⟨⟨h_irrefl, h_trans, h_tricho⟩, h_parent⟩ := hTop
    refine ⟨⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩, ?_⟩
    ·
      intro v hv
      exact h_irrefl v (mem_of_mem_hardInterventionOn hv)
    ·
      intro u hu v hv w hw hluv hlvw
      exact h_trans u (mem_of_mem_hardInterventionOn hu)
                    v (mem_of_mem_hardInterventionOn hv)
                    w (mem_of_mem_hardInterventionOn hw)
                    hluv hlvw
    ·
      intro v hv w hw
      exact h_tricho v (mem_of_mem_hardInterventionOn hv)
                     w (mem_of_mem_hardInterventionOn hw)
    ·

      intro v w hvPa
      obtain ⟨hv_G', hvw_E'⟩ := hvPa
      have hvw_E_G : (v, w) ∈ G.E := (Finset.mem_filter.mp hvw_E').1
      have hv_G : v ∈ G := mem_of_mem_hardInterventionOn hv_G'
      exact h_parent v w ⟨hv_G, hvw_E_G⟩
lean_explanation:
The theorem `acyclic_preserved_under_do` takes a CDMG `G : CDMG Node` over an arbitrary vertex type `Node` equipped with decidable equality (`[DecidableEq Node]`, inherited from the underlying `CDMG` definition where it powers `Finset`-backed membership and filter operations). Two further explicit arguments package the intervention: `W : Finset Node` is the set of intervened nodes, and `hW : W ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V` is the side condition that `W` lies in the node carrier — exactly the LN's bound $W \subseteq J \cup V$. The conclusion is the implication `G.IsAcyclic → (a) ∧ (b)`, where (a) asserts `(G.hardInterventionOn W hW).IsAcyclic` and (b) is the universally-quantified conditional `∀ lt : Node → Node → Prop, G.IsTopologicalOrder lt → (G.hardInterventionOn W hW).IsTopologicalOrder lt`. Consumers reach `.1` for the acyclicity-preservation conclusion and `.2` for the topological-order-preservation conclusion.

The Mathlib idioms worth flagging are mostly `Finset` machinery. `G.J`, `G.V`, `G.E`, and `W` are all `Finset`s rather than `Set`s, which keeps the underlying CDMG definitions algorithmically computable. Membership statements reduce to `Finset.mem_union`, `Finset.mem_sdiff`, and `Finset.mem_filter`, and the proof's case analyses dispatch them with `Finset.mem_union_left` / `Finset.mem_union_right`. The expression `v ∈ G` itself is sugar for a `Membership Node (CDMG Node)` instance from the CDMG definition, which unfolds to `v ∈ G.J ∪ G.V`. In the topological-order branch, the four conjuncts of `IsTopologicalOrder` (irreflexivity, transitivity, trichotomy, and parent-precedence) are exposed all at once via the nested destructure `obtain ⟨⟨h_irrefl, h_trans, h_tricho⟩, h_parent⟩ := hTop`.

The encoding shape is a single theorem head taking the CDMG and the intervention data, returning a conjunction `(a) ∧ (b)` under the shared antecedent `G.IsAcyclic`. The proof body splits via `refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩`. Sub-claim (a) lifts a hypothetical non-trivial closed directed walk in `G.hardInterventionOn W hW` back to a non-trivial closed directed walk in `G` (the same tuple of vertices and edges, by the edge-inclusion `(G.hardInterventionOn W hW).E ⊆ G.E` and the carrier-matching equality), contradicting `G.IsAcyclic`. Sub-claim (b) transfers each of the four `IsTopologicalOrder` conjuncts: the three strict-total-order conjuncts use only the carrier-matching equality (their universally-quantified vertices range over the same set $J \cup V$), while parent-precedence uses the pointwise parent-set inclusion $\Pa^{G_{\doit(W)}}(w) \subseteq \Pa^{G}(w)$ that falls out of `Finset.mem_filter`.
design_choices:
**One theorem returning a conjunction, not two.** The LN remark joins two clauses with "and": acyclicity is preserved, and every topological order is preserved. The Lean rendering returns `(a) ∧ (b)` under the shared antecedent `G.IsAcyclic`, so consumers reach `.1` for acyclicity-preservation and `.2` for topological-order-preservation. Splitting into two top-level theorems was rejected because it would duplicate the `G.IsAcyclic` hypothesis at both theorem heads and obscure the LN's "and"-packaging behind two separate grep targets.

**Sub-claim (b) is universally quantified over `lt`.** The LN's indefinite article in "a topological order for $G$ is also one for $G_{\doit(W)}$" is read universally — *every* such order, not merely some such. An existential rendering ($\exists\, <$) would be vacuous: any acyclic CDMG already admits *some* topological order by the chapter's earlier "acyclic iff topological order" equivalence, so once (a) delivers acyclicity of the intervened CDMG, an existential conclusion follows for free. The universal reading carries the actual content: every relation that tops `G` continues to top `G.hardInterventionOn W hW`, with no need to re-pick.

**Same `lt` on both sides; no re-indexing function.** The node carriers of `G` and `G.hardInterventionOn W hW` coincide as sets — the carrier-matching equality $J_{\doit(W)} \cup V_{\doit(W)} = J \cup V$ follows from items i. and ii. of the hard-intervention definition together with the precondition $W \subseteq J \cup V$. So a binary relation on $J \cup V$ is the very same binary relation on the carrier of the intervened CDMG. In Lean, `lt : Node → Node → Prop` has a type independent of which CDMG it is "for"; both `IsTopologicalOrder` predicates consume the same `lt` directly, with the CDMG-dependence carried through the predicate's CDMG argument rather than through `lt`'s domain. No carrier-translation wrapper `lt' u v := lt u v` is introduced — it would be no-op noise at every consumer site.

**Sub-claim (b) actually holds without acyclicity.** The intervened edge set is a subset of the original (`(G.hardInterventionOn W hW).E ⊆ G.E`), so the parents-precede-children clause is monotone under edge removal: every parent in the intervened graph is also a parent in the original, hence still precedes its child under any order that worked for the original. The LN nevertheless packages (a) and (b) under the shared `G.IsAcyclic` antecedent. We mirror that packaging for LN-fidelity; the proof of (b) silently never invokes the acyclicity hypothesis.

**The bidirected-edge set $L$ plays no role.** Both sub-claims concern the directed-edge skeleton only: acyclicity is defined via the non-existence of non-trivial directed walks (which use only $E$), and a topological order is defined via the parent set (also built from $E$). Neither reads $L$. Whatever choice is made about how the bidirected-edge component transforms under hard intervention is invisible to this claim's statement and proof.
